title: The Big Trip
learningGoal: The learner will decode a predictive narrative saturated with 'will' and 'going to', actively recognizing the inviolable use of the bare infinitive following future auxiliaries.
instructions: Read this story about a family planning a big vacation for tomorrow. Look carefully at how they talk about the future. They use the word 'WILL'. Notice that after 'WILL', the action word is completely clean! No '-ing'! Answer the 8 questions.

contentItems.text: Also notice the phrase 'Going to'. Native speakers use this to talk about a fixed plan they already made for the future.

readingText: Tomorrow is a very big day. My family is going to travel to London. We are very excited. Tonight, I will pack my red bag. I will put my clothes in the bag. I will not sleep late. Tomorrow morning, we will wake up at five o'clock. My father will drive the car to the airport. The airport is far away. We will arrive at the airport at six o'clock. We are going to drink coffee there. Then, we will get on the big airplane. The airplane will fly very fast. I will sit near the window. I will look at the clouds. My brother will probably sleep on the airplane. He is very lazy. We will fly for ten hours. When we arrive in London, it will be cold. I will wear my new coat. We are going to stay in a nice hotel. On the first day, we will walk in the city. We will see the big clock. We will eat hot food. I think we will have a great time on our vacation. I am going to take many photographs.
textInTargetLanguage: Tomorrow is a very big day. My family is going to travel to London. We are very excited. Tonight, I will pack my red bag. I will put my clothes in the bag. I will not sleep late. Tomorrow morning, we will wake up at five o'clock. My father will drive the car to the airport. The airport is far away. We will arrive at the airport at six o'clock. We are going to drink coffee there. Then, we will get on the big airplane. The airplane will fly very fast. I will sit near the window. I will look at the clouds. My brother will probably sleep on the airplane. He is very lazy. We will fly for ten hours. When we arrive in London, it will be cold. I will wear my new coat. We are going to stay in a nice hotel. On the first day, we will walk in the city. We will see the big clock. We will eat hot food. I think we will have a great time on our vacation. I am going to take many photographs.
baseLanguageSupportText: कल बहुत बड़ा दिन है। मेरा परिवार लंदन की यात्रा करने जा रहा है। हम बहुत उत्साहित हैं। आज रात, मैं अपना लाल बैग पैक करूँगा। मैं अपने कपड़े बैग में रखूँगा। मैं देर तक नहीं सोऊँगा। कल सुबह, हम पाँच बजे उठेंगे। मेरे पिता कार को हवाई अड्डे तक चलाएंगे। हवाई अड्डा दूर है। हम छह बजे हवाई अड्डे पर पहुँचेंगे। हम वहां कॉफी पीने वाले हैं। फिर, हम बड़े हवाई जहाज पर चढ़ेंगे। हवाई जहाज बहुत तेज़ी से उड़ेगा। मैं खिड़की के पास बैठूँगा। मैं बादलों को देखूँगा। मेरा भाई शायद हवाई जहाज पर सोएगा। वह बहुत आलसी है। हम दस घंटे उड़ान भरेंगे। जब हम लंदन पहुँचेंगे, तो ठंड होगी। मैं अपना नया कोट पहनूँगा। हम एक अच्छे होटल में ठहरने वाले हैं। पहले दिन, हम शहर में घूमेंगे। हम बड़ी घड़ी देखेंगे। हम गर्म खाना खाएंगे। मुझे लगता है कि हम अपनी छुट्टी पर बहुत मज़ा करेंगे। मैं कई तस्वीरें लेने वाला हूँ।

glossary.baseTranslation: जा रहा है / करने वाला है

exampleSentence.targetText: My family is going to travel.
exampleSentence.transliteration: माई फैमिली इज़ गोइंग टू ट्रेवल।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: मेरा परिवार यात्रा करने जा रहा है।

exampleSentence.targetText: I will sit near the window.
exampleSentence.transliteration: आई विल सिट नियर द विंडो।

exampleSentence.targetText: I will wear my new coat.
exampleSentence.transliteration: आई विल वेयर माई न्यू कोट।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Tonight, I will pack my red bag.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: टुनाइट, आई विल पैक माई रेड बैग।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: आज रात, मैं अपना लाल बैग पैक करूँगा। (Will is followed by the clean verb 'pack')

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: We are going to drink coffee there.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: वी आर गोइंग टू ड्रिंक कॉफी देयर।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: हम वहां कॉफी पीने वाले हैं। (Going to + clean verb)
मेरा भाई शायद सोएगा। ('Sleep' has no 's' at the end, even though 'brother' is one person, because 'will' protects the verb!)

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: We will have a great time.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: वी विल हैव अ ग्रेट टाइम।

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Look at the sentence: 'I will pack my bag.' Are there any '-ed' or '-ing' letters after the word 'pack'?
What time is the family going to arrive at the airport?
What are they planning to do at the airport? (We are going to ____ coffee).
Where will the writer sit on the airplane?
True or False: The text says 'My father will drives the car.' (With an 's' on drive).
What will the writer wear in London?
The text says the brother 'will probably _____'. What is the clean verb?
At the very end of the story, what is the writer 'going to' take?

expectedSummaryPoints: The learner correlates anticipation of the future with the modal 'will'.
The learner inherently visualizes that 'will' acts as a morphological shield, preventing any suffixes on the subsequent root verb.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You correctly identified the future events and perfectly noticed how clean the verbs are after 'will'!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember, 'Will' tells us something is happening tomorrow. And it wipes the verb clean. WILL WALK. WILL EAT.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Just remember that 'will' destroys all other rules. Even if it is 'He' or 'My father', you don't use an 'S'. 'My father will drive' is correct. 'My father will drives' is completely wrong!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Tomorrow is a very big day... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Look closely at the verbs after 'Will'. Notice how 'He' does not force 'Sleep' to turn into 'Sleeps'. The 'Will' protects it!
commonMistakes: Adding 's' to third-person singular verbs after 'will'.
